Title: Salmonella enterica exploits the auxin signaling pathway to overcome stomatal immunity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for STm 14028s-triggered stomatal reopening; loss of AUX1 impairs auxin-dependent stomatal susceptibility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using aux1-7; stomatal bioassays; pharmacological inhibition with 2-NOA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcriptomic data indicated modulation by STm 14028s at 4 HPI
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening and helps Salmonella overcome stomatal immunity
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf inoculation with Salmonella enterica serovar Typhimurium 14028s; 4 HPI; auxin transport inhibitor 2-NOA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AUX1 is part of the Arabidopsis auxin pathway modulated by STm 14028s. 2. The aux1-7 mutant showed impaired stomatal reopening in response to STm 14028s at 4 HPI. 3. Blocking auxin uptake with 2-NOA similarly disrupted full stomatal reopening, supporting AUX1-dependent auxin signaling in this phenotype.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; metabolomics dataset MetaboLights MTBLS9884
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pst DC3000 still reopened stomata in aux1-7, indicating AUX1 requirement is specific to the Salmonella-auxin mechanism rather than general stomatal reopening.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RUB/NEDD8-activating enzyme subunit involved in SCF-mediated auxin signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full STm 14028s-triggered stomatal reopening; mutation reduces stomatal susceptibility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using axr1-3; stomatal bioassays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcriptomic data indicated auxin signaling pathway modulation by STm 14028s
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening and helps Salmonella overcome stomatal immunity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AXR1-dependent auxin signaling is needed for stomatal susceptibility to STm 14028s. 2. axr1-3 stomata closed normally at 2 HPI but failed to reopen like wild type at 4 HPI. 3. Pst DC3000 still reopened axr1-3 stomata, indicating pathway specificity for Salmonella-associated auxin action.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; metabolomics dataset MetaboLights MTBLS9884
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Stomatal morphology of axr1-3 was not significantly different from Col-0, excluding developmental artifacts.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Typhimurium
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates indole-3-pyruvate to indole-3-acetaldehyde in the bacterial IAA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor of the terminal product IAA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme for auxin biosynthesis in Salmonella, as stated in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Bacterial ipdC promotes IAA production and contributes to stomatal reopening in Arabidopsis and lettuce; deletion causes only partial reopening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant (STm 14028s ΔipdC); heterologous expression in E. coli O157:H7; IAA quantification in culture and phyllosphere; stomatal bioassays; exogenous IAA complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated in STm 14028s after 4 h exposure to Arabidopsis and lettuce leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening, increases bacterial fitness, and helps overcome stomatal immunity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf surface interaction at 4 HPI; tryptophan supplementation in M9 medium; exogenous IAA supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Heterologous expression in E. coli O157:H7 from pET28a-derived construct; promoter not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ipdC was significantly upregulated when STm 14028s contacted Arabidopsis or lettuce leaves. 2. STm 14028s ΔipdC lost full IAA production and only partially reopened stomata. 3. Heterologous expression of ipdC in E. coli O157:H7 conferred partial stomatal reopening and restored bacterial IAA production in vitro. 4. Exogenous IAA complemented the stomatal reopening defect, supporting ipdC-mediated auxin as a causal factor.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ipdC reduces virulence/fitness-related traits; in this study deletion reduced capacity to overcome stomatal immunity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; IAA ELISA assays; metabolomics dataset MetaboLights MTBLS9884
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting bacterial auxin biosynthesis or blocking host perception of bacterium-derived auxin could reduce pathogen internalization into edible leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ipdC alone was insufficient for full stomatal reopening in the O157:H7 background because this bacterium did not induce plant auxin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Typhimurium
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of indole-glycerol phosphate-derived indole intermediate in tryptophan biosynthesis as part of tryptophan synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for IAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated bacterial tryptophan biosynthesis likely supports auxin production during leaf interaction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic data mining correlated with exometabolomic accumulation of tryptophan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated in STm 14028s exposed to Arabidopsis leaves at 4 HPI
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening and contributes to overcoming stomatal immunity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure of STm 14028s to Arabidopsis leaves; 4 HPI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. trpA was significantly upregulated in STm 14028s during Arabidopsis interaction. 2. Leaf exudates inoculated with STm 14028s showed elevated tryptophan accumulation. 3. These observations support enhanced precursor supply for bacterial auxin biosynthesis during stomatal reopening.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; metabolomics dataset MetaboLights MTBLS9884
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target to restrict bacterial auxin precursor supply on leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not directly mutate trpA in this work; evidence is transcriptomic and metabolomic rather than direct genetics.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative conversion of indole-3-pyruvic acid toward indole-3-acetic acid biosynthesis in the YUCCA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IAA pathway intermediate leading directly to indole-3-acetic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the terminal product pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point inferred for Arabidopsis auxin biosynthesis in this interaction; YUC enzymes inhibited by YDF reduced phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Plant YUC-mediated auxin biosynthesis, likely through YUC5, contributes to STm 14028s-induced stomatal reopening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; RT-qPCR validation; pharmacological inhibition with yucasin-DF followed by stomatal bioassays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: YUC5 was significantly upregulated by STm 14028s and other human pathogenic strains, but not by Pst DC3000
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Arabidopsis leaves at 4 HPI after STm 14028s inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening and susceptibility to Salmonella entry
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: STm 14028s inoculation at 4 HPI; YDF pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC5 was the only tested Arabidopsis YUCCA gene specifically upregulated by human pathogenic bacterial strains. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed YUC5 induction at 4 HPI. 3. Inhibiting YUCCA activity with yucasin-DF reduced STm 14028s-induced stomatal reopening, implicating plant YUC-dependent auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Blocking YUCCA activity may affect endogenous auxin-dependent growth processes, though no immediate effect on basal stomatal aperture was observed after YDF pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; RT-qPCR validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating guard-cell or leaf-surface auxin biosynthesis could reduce Salmonella internalization, but may require tissue-specific strategies to avoid developmental auxin defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states Arabidopsis auxin biosynthesis is induced likely through YUC5; evidence is strong but based on expression and inhibitor data rather than a yuc5 mutant.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Auxin perception through SCF(TIR1)-mediated signaling leading to degradation of Aux/IAA repressors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates auxin-responsive transcription indirectly via Aux/IAA degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential auxin signaling component required for STm 14028s-triggered stomatal reopening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TIR1 disrupts Salmonella-induced stomatal reopening despite normal early stomatal closure
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using tir1-10; pharmacological inhibition with auxinole and MG132; stomatal bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin signaling genes including TIR1 and AFB/ARF/IAA family members were modulated by STm 14028s in transcriptomic data
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal reopening and facilitates Salmonella entry through stomata
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: STm 14028s inoculation at 4 HPI; auxinole and MG132 pretreatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: IAA accumulation from bacterial and plant biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption of auxin receptor function can broadly impair plant development, though stomatal morphology was not altered in tir1-10 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TIR1-dependent auxin perception is required for full stomatal reopening by STm 14028s. 2. tir1-10 stomata closed normally at 2 HPI but failed to reopen to wild-type levels at 4 HPI. 3. Pharmacological inhibition of SCF(TIR1) signaling with auxinole or proteasome inhibition with MG132 phenocopied the defect.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Dual transcriptomics data mined from Jacob et al. [33]; metabolomics dataset MetaboLights MTBLS9884
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial or localized suppression of auxin perception in leaves/guard cells could be explored to limit Salmonella internalization, but whole-plant disruption may cause major developmental penalties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pst DC3000 still reopened tir1-10 stomata, showing that TIR1 dependence is specific to the Salmonella auxin-based mechanism rather than general stomatal reopening.
